# hyperfluid_key_value_cache (Data Source)

Look up an existing key-value cache by name within an environment.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "hyperfluid_env" "default" {
  name = "default"
}

data "hyperfluid_key_value_cache" "sessions" {
  env  = data.hyperfluid_env.default.id
  name = "sessions"
}

output "cache_host" {
  value = data.hyperfluid_key_value_cache.sessions.host
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `env` (String) Environment id the cache runs in.
- `name` (String) Cache name.

### Read-Only

- `credentials_secret_name` (String) Secret holding the connection credentials.
- `description` (String) Free-form description.
- `host` (String) Connection host.
- `id` (String) Cache id.
- `image` (String) Cache image.
- `maxmemory` (String) Max memory (e.g. 256mb).
- `maxmemory_policy` (String) Eviction policy.
- `phase` (String) Current lifecycle phase.
- `port` (Number) Connection port.
- `slug` (String) Derived slug.
- `tags` (List of String) User-defined tags.
